questions.prompt: Which Claude tier should be your starting default?

choices.text: Sonnet — strong general intelligence at fraction of Opus cost. Move down to Haiku for cheap simple tasks, up to Opus for hard reasoning, only when there's a reason.

questions.explanation: Sonnet is the production default. Always-Opus is wasteful; always-Haiku misses nuance. Mixed-tier routing — easy → Haiku, hard → Opus — is the biggest cost lever after caching.

questions.prompt: Where does the system prompt go in the Messages API?

choices.text: As a top-level `system` field on the request, separate from `messages`. The messages array contains only user/assistant turns, must alternate, and must start with user.

questions.explanation: Claude's API has a dedicated `system` field at the top level — this is the most common mistake for engineers migrating from OpenAI.

questions.prompt: You're storing only `response.content[0].text` in your agent's history. Tool calls keep repeating. What's wrong?

choices.text: Tool use isn't supported in Sonnet.

`response.content` is an array of typed blocks — text, tool_use, image, etc. Storing only the first block loses tool_use data, so on the next turn Claude doesn't know it already called the tool. Always append the full `response.content` array to history.

questions.explanation: Multi-block responses are how Claude returns tool calls alongside text. Persist the entire `content` array — never strip blocks before appending to history.

questions.prompt: Which is the strongest pattern for passing user-supplied content to Claude?

choices.text: Concatenate everything in one big string.

Wrap untrusted content in XML tags like `<user_input>...</user_input>` and tell Claude in the system prompt to treat tagged content as data, not instructions. Reduces prompt injection risk and lets you reference content unambiguously elsewhere in the prompt.
Put it in the system prompt.

questions.explanation: XML tags are Anthropic's recommended pattern. Claude is specifically trained on them; they delimit content cleanly and harden the prompt against injection attempts hidden inside user data.

questions.prompt: A 60K-token knowledge base is being stuffed into every Claude call. Bill is 5× budget. Best fix?

choices.text: Switch to RAG: embed the corpus, retrieve only the 2–5K tokens relevant to each query, pass those instead. Stack with prompt caching on the system prompt for additional savings. Typical reduction: 90%+ input cost with no quality loss.

questions.explanation: Stuffing entire corpora is the most common Claude cost mistake. RAG retrieves only relevant chunks; caching discounts repeat content; both stack. Module 4 covers contextual retrieval, Anthropic's RAG upgrade that drops failures 40–67%.
